perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that (i) the documents provided to the Receiving Party hereunder containing Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party shall be used by the Receiving Party solely for the purpose of evaluating its interest in the business arrangements described or performing a future agreement between the Parties; (ii) it will not use such documents disclosed hereunder for any other purpose; and (iii) it will not distribute, disclose or disseminate Proprietary Information to anyone except its 1 <PAGE> employees with a need to know who are involved in the consideration or performance of the business arrangements described herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that (i) the documents provided to the Receiving Party hereunder containing Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party shall be used by the Receiving Party for evaluating its interest in the business arrangements described or performing a future agreement between the Parties; (ii) it will not use such documents disclosed hereunder for any other purpose; and (iii) it will not distribute, disclose or disseminate Proprietary Information to anyone except its employees.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'with a need to know who are involved in the consideration or performance of the business arrangements described herein' is removed, allowing the Receiving Party to disclose Proprietary Information to any employee without restriction. This change is a violation to Section 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall have, or shall enter into, agreements with its parent, divisions, subsidiary companies and consultants that will safeguard the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder consistent with the terms of this Agreement. With respect to employees, the Receiving Party shall advise all employees who will have access to Proprietary Information as to their obligations contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the dissemination of Proprietary Information to only employees with a 'need to know who are involved in the consideration or performance of the business arrangements described herein'. The changed text removes this restriction, allowing dissemination to any employee. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the Receiving Party to have agreements with its parent, divisions, subsidiary companies and consultants, and advise *all* employees who will have access to Proprietary Information as to their obligations to safeguard the information, implying not all employees may have access initially. The changed text's allowance of unrestricted employee access conflicts with the implication in the contradicted text that access should be controlled and obligations communicated, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each party may disclose any Proprietary Information to the extent that such Party has been advised by counsel that such disclosure is necessary to comply with laws or regulations, or any judicial order or request, or as necessary in connection with the filing of any patent, copyright, or similar application or registration; provided that each Party shall give the other Party reasonable advance notice of such proposed disclosure, shall where practicable use its best efforts to secure confidential treatment of any such Proprietary Information and shall advise the other Party in writing of the manner of the disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party may disclose any Proprietary Information to the extent that such Party has been advised by counsel that such disclosure is necessary to comply with laws or regulations, or any judicial order or request, or as necessary in connection with the filing of any patent, copyright, or similar application or registration; provided that each Party shall give the other Party reasonable advance notice of such proposed disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The clause 'shall where practicable use its best efforts to secure confidential treatment of any such Proprietary Information and shall advise the other Party in writing of the manner of the disclosure' is removed, meaning that a party is not legally compelled to make efforts to treat the information as confidential when disclosing it. This makes the paragraph inconsistent with paragraph 3, which is a general provision about protecting proprietary information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party shall be treated and safeguarded hereunder by the Receiving Party for a period determined by the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party warrants that it applies reasonable safeguards against the unauthorized disclosure of Proprietary Information and that it will protect such Proprietary Information as least as securely as it protects its own Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text required a party disclosing Proprietary Information to 'use its best efforts to secure confidential treatment' and 'advise the other Party in writing of the manner of the disclosure'. The changed text removes this requirement. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which states 'Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party shall be treated and safeguarded hereunder by the Receiving Party' and requires the Receiving Party to 'protect such Proprietary Information as least as securely as it protects its own Proprietary Information'. By removing the obligation to secure confidential treatment during disclosure, the changed text undermines the explicit protection of Proprietary Information mandated elsewhere in the contract, creating a strong and direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any amendment to this Agreement must be in writing and signed by authorized officials of each Party. No failure or delay in exercising any right under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Any amendment to this Agreement must be in writing and signed by authorized officials of each Party.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'No failure or delay in exercising any right under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof.' implies that a failure to act promptly *can* now be interpreted as a waiver, contradicting any sections where one party expects to enforce its rights after a delay. Particularly, this affects the right to demand the return of proprietary information in Section 11.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At the Disclosing Party's request, all Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party in tangible form that is in the possession of the Receiving Party shall be returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained a clause stating that failure to exercise a right does not waive that right. The changed text removes this clause. The Perturbation Explanation states this removal implies that failure to act promptly *can* now be interpreted as a waiver. The Contradicted Text gives a party the right to demand the return of proprietary information. If this right is not immediately exercised, the removal of the waiver clause could imply that the party has waived its right to demand the information back, directly contradicting the right itself. Therefore, a strong contradiction is introduced.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Maryland.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the jurisdiction chosen by the receiving party.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the state of Maryland as the governing law with the jursidiction chosen by the receiving party, it opens the possibility for the receiving party to chose a different jurisdiction, that could permit other contradictory actions, and potentially render the protection of certain information to be null. This action contradicts the definition of Proprietary Information in section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As Used Herein: "Information" is defined as communications or data including, but not limited to, business information, marketing plans, technical or financial information, customer lists or proposals, sketches, models, samples, computer programs and documentation, drawings, specifications, whether conveyed in oral, written, graphic, or electromagnetic form or otherwise. "Party" is defined as either entity executing this Agreement and any subsidiary, division, affiliate, or parent company of such entity. "Proprietary Information" is defined as that Information owned or possessed by either Party that said Party desires to protect as confidential against unrestricted disclosure or improper competitive use, and that is designated as such in the manner provided by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies Maryland law. The changed text allows the receiving party to choose the governing jurisdiction. If the receiving party chooses a jurisdiction with weaker protection for proprietary information, it directly contradicts the intent and definition of 'Proprietary Information' in the contradicted text, as the changed text creates a scenario where the 'Proprietary Information' may not be protected as intended, creating a strong in-text contradiction based on the given explanation.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall have, or shall enter into, agreements with its parent, divisions, subsidiary companies and consultants that will safeguard the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder consistent with the terms of this Agreement. With respect to employees, the Receiving Party shall advise all employees who will have access to Proprietary Information as to their obligations contained herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall encourage its parent, divisions, subsidiary companies and consultants to safeguard the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder consistent with the terms of this Agreement. With respect to employees, the Receiving Party shall advise all employees who will have access to Proprietary Information as to their obligations contained herein.
perturbation.explanation: The word 'have or enter into agreements' that safeguard the propietary information has been replaced with 'encourage', creating an in-text contradiction. The contradiction is specifically created because it removes the word that legally holds the recieving party accountable, thus contradicting the entire reason for a confidentiality agreement. This action contradicts the meaning of Proprietary information in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As Used Herein: "Information" is defined as communications or data including, but not limited to, business information, marketing plans, technical or financial information, customer lists or proposals, sketches, models, samples, computer programs and documentation, drawings, specifications, whether conveyed in oral, written, graphic, or electromagnetic form or otherwise. "Party" is defined as either entity executing this Agreement and any subsidiary, division, affiliate, or parent company of such entity. "Proprietary Information" is defined as that Information owned or possessed by either Party that said Party desires to protect as confidential against unrestricted disclosure or improper competitive use, and that is designated as such in the manner provided by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the Receiving Party to 'have, or shall enter into, agreements' to safeguard Proprietary Information. The changed text weakens this obligation to merely 'encourage' such safeguards. This directly contradicts the definition of 'Proprietary Information' in the contradicted text, which emphasizes the desire to protect confidential information against unrestricted disclosure. By removing the legally binding requirement to have agreements in place, the changed text undermines the protection of Proprietary Information, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
